bibliographic_data.title: TOI-824 b: A New Planet on the Lower Edge of the Hot Neptune Desert
bibliographic_data.abstract:
We report the detection of a transiting hot Neptune exoplanet orbiting TOI-824 (SCR J1448-5735), a nearby (d = 64 pc) K4V star, using data from the Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite (TESS). The newly discovered planet has a radius, Rp = 2.93 ± 0.20 R⊕, and an orbital period of 1.393 days. Radial velocity measurements using the Planet Finder Spectrograph (PFS) and the High Accuracy Radial velocity Planet Searcher (HARPS) spectrograph confirm the existence of the planet and we estimate its mass to be 18.47 ± 1.84 M⊕. The planet’s mean density is ρp = 4.03+0.98
−0.78 g cm−3, making it more than twice as dense as Neptune. TOI-824 b’s high equilibrium temperature makes the planet likely to have a cloud free atmosphere, and thus an excellent candidate for follow up atmospheric studies. The detectability of TOI-824 b’s atmosphere from both ground and space is promising and could lead to the detailed characterization of the most irradiated, small planet at the edge of the hot Neptune desert that has retained its atmosphere to date.

core_content.novelty_claim: Detection and characterization of a new transiting exoplanet, TOI-824 b, which is the most irradiated small planet at the edge of the hot Neptune desert that has retained its atmosphere.
core_content.key_methodology: The detection was made using data from the Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite (TESS) and confirmed with radial velocity measurements from the Planet Finder Spectrograph (PFS) and High Accuracy Radial velocity Planet Searcher (HARPS).
outcomes: Detection of a new transiting exoplanet, TOI-824 b
Estimation of its mass and density
Confirmation that it is an excellent candidate for atmospheric studies
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper reports the detection of a new transiting exoplanet orbiting TOI-824 (SCR J1448-5735), a nearby K4V star. The planet, named TOI-824 b, has a radius of 2.93 ± 0.20 R⊕ and an orbital period of 1.393 days. Radial velocity measurements using the Planet Finder Spectrograph (PFS) and the High Accuracy Radial velocity Planet Searcher (HARPS) confirmed its existence and estimated its mass to be 18.47 ± 1.84 M⊕. The planet’s high equilibrium temperature suggests it may have a cloud-free atmosphere, making it an excellent candidate for atmospheric studies. This discovery is significant as TOI-824 b is the most irradiated small planet at the edge of the hot Neptune desert that has retained its atmosphere to date.
